rationale: In the theory section, the authors explicitly cite Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) when defining utilitarian versus hedonic products, and this distinction is used to derive H3 and structure later analyses. Product type is then operationalized and tested across studies as part of the model of review helpfulness. Although the citation is grouped with another source and does not use Dhar and Wertenbroch's specific choice mechanism, it serves a clear conceptual function beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-versus-utilitarian product distinction as a moderator framework in a different context, namely judgments of online review helpfulness. It transfers that conceptual categorization into its hypothesis development and empirical design.
